agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Memory leak from undisposed WebSocket listeners on courier disconnect/reconnect will OOM the service; unguarded JSON.parse will crash the process on malformed messages."
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Memory leak: WebSocket listeners never removed on disconnect",
      "risk": "Each courier reconnect creates a new CourierTracker with new listeners, but the old instance's listeners are never removed and the old instance is never dereferenced. With 'average session: 5 minutes', each courier creates ~288 leaked listener closures per day. The closures capture `this` (the CourierTracker), which holds a reference to the WebSocket and courierId, preventing GC. This explains the 3-month steady memory increase.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "CourierTracker.setupListeners()",
      "reproduction": "1. Connect courier A → creates CourierTracker #1 with listeners on ws. 2. Courier A disconnects (ws closes) → no cleanup runs, #1 stays in memory. 3. Courier A reconnects → creates CourierTracker #2. 4. Repeat 288x/day. Monitor process.memoryUsage().heapUsed — it grows monotonically.",
      "fix": "Add a `close` listener that removes all listeners and nulls references. Add a `destroy()` method. Example:\n\n```typescript\nprivate setupListeners(): void {\n  const onMessage = (data: string) => { ... };\n  const onError = (err: Error) => { ... };\n  const onClose = () => {\n    this.ws.off('message', onMessage);\n    this.ws.off('error', onError);\n    this.ws.off('close', onClose);\n  };\n  this.ws.on('message', onMessage);\n  this.ws.on('error', onError);\n  this.ws.on('close', onClose);\n}\n```",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Service stability / uptime SLA"
    },
    {
      "title": "Unguarded JSON.parse crashes process on malformed WebSocket message",
      "risk": "A single malformed message from any courier (network corruption, client bug, or malicious payload) throws a SyntaxError inside the 'message' listener. If there is no top-level uncaughtException handler, this kills the Node process for ALL couriers — not just the offending one.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "CourierTracker.setupListeners() → ws.on('message', ...)",
      "reproduction": "Send a non-JSON string over the WebSocket (e.g. raw bytes from a network glitch). JSON.parse throws SyntaxError. Process crashes.",
      "fix": "Wrap in try/catch:\n```typescript\nthis.ws.on('message', (data: string) => {\n  let location;\n  try {\n    location = JSON.parse(data);\n  } catch {\n    console.warn(`Courier ${this.courierId}: invalid message, dropping`);\n    return;\n  }\n  if (typeof location?.lat !== 'number' || typeof location?.lng !== 'number') return;\n  this.updateLocation(location);\n});\n```",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Service availability — single bad message should not take down the entire service"
    },
    {
      "title": "Fire-and-forget database write with no error handling",
      "risk": "updateLocation calls db.couriers.updateLocation without await or .catch(). If the DB is slow or down: (1) unhandled promise rejections will eventually crash Node (--unhandled-rejections=throw is default since Node 15), (2) no backpressure — during DB slowdowns, unbounded promises queue in memory compounding the leak, (3) location updates are silently lost with no logging or retry.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "CourierTracker.updateLocation()",
      "reproduction": "Simulate DB latency (add 5s delay). Send rapid location updates. Observe: unhandled rejection warnings, memory spike from queued promises, lost updates with zero logging.",
      "fix": "```typescript\nprivate async updateLocation(location: { lat: number; lng: number }): Promise<void> {\n  try {\n    await db.couriers.updateLocation(this.courierId, location.lat, location.lng);\n  } catch (err) {\n    console.error(`Courier ${this.courierId} DB write failed:`, err);\n    // emit metric, optionally buffer/retry\n  }\n}\n```",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data integrity — courier locations must actually persist"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No input validation on lat/lng",
      "description": "location.lat and location.lng are assumed to be numbers but never validated. A payload like {\"lat\": \"foo\", \"lng\": null} will write garbage to the database. Should validate type and range (-90..90 lat, -180..180 lng).",
      "location": "CourierTracker.updateLocation()"
    },
    {
      "title": "No rate limiting on location updates",
      "description": "A misbehaving or compromised courier client can flood the WebSocket with updates, hammering the DB with unbounded writes. Should throttle to e.g. 1 update/second per courier.",
      "location": "CourierTracker.setupListeners()"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Add a heartbeat/ping-pong mechanism to detect zombie connections earlier than TCP timeout",
    "Log courier connect/disconnect events with session duration for operational visibility",
    "Consider batching or debouncing DB writes — last-known-location only needs the most recent value, not every intermediate point"
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "The memory leak is already in production and worsening. Fix should be deployed urgently. No migration needed — purely behavioral change.",
    "rollback_capability": "Safe to roll back — fix only adds cleanup logic and error handling. No state changes.",
    "backward_compatibility": "No breaking changes. WebSocket protocol and DB schema unchanged.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No metrics or structured logging present. Add: connection count gauge, message parse error rate, DB write error rate, heap usage.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert commit. Memory leak returns but service was surviving (albeit degrading) for 3 months."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 95,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Memory leak from missing WebSocket close/cleanup — textbook Node.js EventEmitter leak pattern",
      "JSON.parse crash — deterministic failure on any non-JSON input",
      "Unhandled promise rejection from fire-and-forget DB call"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether there is an external connection manager that might partially mitigate the leak (not visible in provided code)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "No external process manager is restarting the service on OOM (which would mask but not fix the leak)",
      "db.couriers.updateLocation returns a Promise",
      "Node.js version >= 15 (unhandled rejections throw by default)"
    ]
  }
}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this code for production risks.

Key issues I can see immediately:

1. **Memory leak**: No cleanup of WebSocket listeners or tracker instances. When couriers disconnect and reconnect, new CourierTracker instances are created but old ones are never cleaned up. The WebSocket listeners keep references alive, preventing GC.

2. **No JSON.parse error handling**: Malformed WebSocket messages will throw and crash the process.

3. **No await/error handling on db call**: The `updateLocation` call has no error handling - fire and forget with no catch.

Let me focus on the critical ones.
